nonBlockingIssues.description: Reviewer flagged that runtime proof still uses `node dist/index.js` rather than a `brew install` + brewed `mors` command. However, a true `brew install` test requires a published tap (circular dependency for pre-release software). The fix adequately proves: (a) `brew ruby` can parse the formula, (b) the built CLI that the formula installs works end-to-end, (c) version alignment is maintained between npm and Homebrew paths. This is the best achievable at repository-level testing. A post-publish `brew install` integration test could be added to CI once the tap is published.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Update mors-worker skill scope to include distribution-install-ux milestone, since the skill was successfully used for all 5 features in this milestone despite SKILL.md limiting it to core-foundation and local-cli-flows.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: All 5 features in distribution-install-ux (distribution-github-npm-install-ready, distribution-setup-shell-command, distribution-homebrew-tap-formula, distribution-install-matrix-and-first-run-flow, distribution-fix-homebrew-runtime-validation-evidence) used skillName: mors-worker. Flagged by 4/5 reviewers as scope drift.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add explicit guidance in mors-worker skill for validation-only / test-only features where new tests may intentionally pass against existing behavior without source changes, relaxing the strict red/green TDD requirement for these cases.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Feature distribution-install-matrix-and-first-run-flow created test/install-matrix.test.ts that passed on first run against existing codebase with no production-code iteration. The strict TDD mandate in SKILL.md step 2 doesn't account for this pattern.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add user-testing guidance for validating installed mors entrypoints from npm global and Homebrew paths, not just local 'node dist/index.js' execution. Specifically, document that repository-level Homebrew validation proves formula parseability and built-CLI functionality, while true `brew install` integration testing should be added post-tap-publication.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: .factory/library/user-testing.md prescribes 'MORS_CONFIG_DIR=... node dist/index.js <command>' for all CLI testing. No guidance exists for verifying installed binary entrypoints. Install-matrix tests now clearly separate static formula checks from runtime executable proof.

rejectedObservations.observation: Shared Homebrew validation guidance now conflates local built-CLI execution with Homebrew-channel runtime proof.
rejectedObservations.reason: The .factory/library/homebrew-formula.md documentation accurately describes the test split (static vs runtime) and explicitly notes that static checks 'do NOT prove that brew install produces a runnable binary'. The runtime section documents brew ruby parsing + CLI execution as the proof path. This is correct factual documentation of the achievable validation approach.

rejectedObservations.observation: Shared CLI user-testing guidance has not been updated to include the new setup-shell command/testing hook used in install UX validation.
rejectedObservations.reason: Redundant with the broader user-testing guidance suggestion already captured in suggestedGuidanceUpdates; setup-shell is adequately covered by its own comprehensive test suite (30 tests in test/setup-shell.test.ts).
